system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleInternalEnergy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   11640.3;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          2.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1.0;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volVectorField;
    object      U;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (10 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (10 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform (10 0 0);
        value           uniform (10 0 0);
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            empty;
    }

    top
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      T;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 0 0 1 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      p;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [1 -1 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.2;

deltaT          0.001;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.01;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  no;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    (-0.15242  0        -0.005)  // 0
    ( 0.3048  0        -0.005)  // 1
    ( 0.3048  0.1524   -0.005)  // 2
    (-0.15242  0.1524   -0.005)  // 3

    (-0.15242  0        0.005)   // 4
    ( 0.3048  0.1524   -0.005)  // 5
    ( 0.3048  0.1524   0.005)   // 6
    (-0.15242  0.1524   0.005)   // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 3 7 4 1 2 6 5) (60 30 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (3 2 6 7 0 1 5 4) (60 30 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 5 1)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (2 3 7 6)
        );
    }

    bottom
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
        );
    }

    top
    {
        type zeroGradient;
        faces
        (
            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }

    wedge
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 2 6 4)
        );
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 7 4)
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Analysis

### Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Trying to specify a boundary face on an internal face

**Error Message:**
```
Trying to specify a boundary face 4(0 4 5 1) on the face on cell 0 which is either an internal face or already belongs to some other patch.  This is face 0 of patch 0 named inlet.
```

This error occurs because **face 4** (the face connecting vertices 0→4, 4→5, 5→1, 1→0) is being claimed by both the `inlet` patch and the `bottom` patch. Looking at the current `blockMeshDict`:

- **Block 0**: `hex (0 3 7 4 1 2 6 5)` — vertices 0,3,7,4 form the bottom face, and 1,2,6,5 form the top face
- **Block 1**: `hex (3 2 6 7 0 1 5 4)` — vertices 3,2,6,7 form the bottom face, and 0,1,5,4 form the top face

The face `(0 4 5 1)` is the **top face of Block 0** and also the **bottom face of Block 1**. Since both blocks share this face internally, it becomes an **internal face**, not a boundary face. The `inlet` patch tries to claim this internal face as its own, which causes the conflict.

Similarly, the `outlet` face `(2 3 7 6)` is the bottom face of Block 1, and the `bottom` patch claims face `(0 1 2 3)` which is the bottom face of Block 0 — these don't conflict. The `top` face `(4 5 6 7)` is shared and thus internal. The `wedge` face `(3 2 6 4)` is unique to Block 1 and should be fine.

### Root Cause

The block topology creates **shared internal faces** between the two blocks. Specifically:
- Face `(0 4 5 1)` is shared by Block 0 (top) and Block 1 (bottom)
- Face `(3 2 6 7)` is shared by Block 0 (bottom) and Block 1 (top)
- Face `(4 5 6 7)` is shared by both blocks (top of Block 0, bottom of Block 1)

Only faces that belong to exactly one block should be listed as boundary patches. Shared/internal faces must be excluded from the boundary specification.

### Proposed Fix

**Modify the `boundary` sub-dictionary in `blockMeshDict`** to remove faces that are shared between blocks (internal faces):

1. **Remove the `bottom` patch entry** — the face `(0 1 2 3)` is the bottom face of Block 0, but Block 1 also has a bottom face `(3 2 6 7)` that shares vertices 3 and 2. Neither of these should be boundary faces since both blocks extend below them. Actually, looking more carefully: Block 0's bottom face `(0 3 7 4)` and Block 1's bottom face `(3 2 6 7)` — neither of these should be boundary faces because both blocks occupy space above them. The true bottom boundary should be the face common to both blocks at the very bottom. This suggests the block topology still needs adjustment.

2. **Better approach**: Restructure the blocks so that one block sits directly on the bottom boundary and the other sits on top of it, eliminating shared bottom/top faces. Define:
   - **Block 0** (lower wedge region): `hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7)` — this block rests on the bottom face `(0 1 2 3)` and extends up to the top face `(4 5 6 7)`. Its front face should be the wedge cut `(3 2 6 4)`.
   - **Block 1** (upper trapezoidal region): `hex (4 5 6 7 0 1 2 3)` — this block sits on top of Block 0, sharing the top face `(4 5 6 7)` and extending upward. Its front face is also the wedge cut `(3 2 6 4)` but from the other side.

However, Block 1 `hex (4 5 6 7 0 1 2 3)` would place vertices 0,1,2,3 at z=-0.005 and 4,5,6,7 at z=+0.005 — this is the same degenerate topology that caused "inside-out" errors in previous attempts.

3. **Best solution**: Keep the current block definitions but **remove ALL shared faces from the boundary list**. Only external faces should be listed:

   - `inlet`: face `(0 4 5 1)` — this is the front face of Block 0, external
   - `outlet`: face `(2 3 7 6)` — this is the back face of Block 1, external  
   - `top`: face `(4 5 6 7)` — this is the top face of Block 1, external
   - `wedge`: face `(3 2 6 4)` — this is the internal wedge face between blocks, but it should remain as a boundary (wall/slip) since it represents the physical wedge surface
   - `bottom`: **REMOVE** — face `(0 1 2 3)` is internal (shared with nothing, but Block 0's actual bottom face is `(0 3 7 4)`)
   - `frontAndBack`: keep as empty (these are the side faces of the extrusion)

Actually, re-examining: Block 0's bottom face is `(0 3 7 4)` and Block 1's bottom face is `(3 2 6 7)`. Neither of these should be boundary faces since both blocks extend above them. The true bottom boundary of the entire domain is the face common to both blocks at the lowest z — but since both blocks share the same bottom extent (z=-0.005), there is no external bottom face. The domain's bottom boundary should be handled via the `frontAndBack` empty patches or the `wedge` face.

The cleanest fix: **Remove the `bottom` patch entirely** since there is no external bottom face — the domain is bounded below by the shared faces of both blocks. The `frontAndBack` empty patches already handle the side boundaries of the extrusion.

**Final recommended boundary list:**
- `inlet`: face `(0 4 5 1)`
- `outlet`: face `(2 3 7 6)`
- `top`: face `(4 5 6 7)`
- `wedge`: face `(3 2 6 4)`
- `frontAndBack`: keep as empty faces

This removes the conflicting `bottom` patch entry and keeps only the truly external faces plus the wedge interface as a wall boundary.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Remove the bottom patch entry entirely since there is no external bottom face; the domain is bounded below by shared faces of both blocks and the frontAndBack empty patches handle the side boundaries'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Perform a 2D supersonic flow simulation over a wedge. Create a 3D geometry spanning from -0.15242 m to 0.3048 m in the x-direction, 0 m to 0.1524 m in the y-direction, and -0.005 m to 0.005 m in the z-direction. The simulation starts at 0.0 seconds and runs until 0.2 seconds with a time step (deltaT) of 0.0001 seconds. The results are written every 0.02 seconds. The inlet velocity is 10 m/s, inlet temperature is 1 K and inlet pressure is 1 Pa. The wedge boundary uses slip boundary condition for velocity. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use rhoCentralFoam solver.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
